description: Provides read-only access to Wikipedia articles, summaries, search results, and related pages via the MediaWiki REST API.

tools.name: search_wikipedia
tools.description: Search Wikipedia for pages matching a query and return a ranked list of results with excerpts and thumbnails.

query.description: The search string used to find matching Wikipedia pages.

limit.description: Maximum number of search results to return (default 5).

language.description: The Wikipedia language code (e.g., 'en' for English) to search within.

query.description: The original search query string echoed back.

results.description: List of matching Wikipedia page summaries.

title.description: The display title of the Wikipedia page.

pageid.description: The unique numeric identifier of the Wikipedia page.

excerpt.description: A short text snippet highlighting the matched query context.

description.description: A concise subtitle or description of the page topic.

thumbnail.description: Thumbnail image metadata (url, width, height) if available.

tools.name: get_article
tools.description: Retrieve the full Wikipedia article content, metadata, and hierarchical section breakdown for a given page title.

title.description: The exact Wikipedia page title whose full article is requested.

language.description: The Wikipedia language code (e.g., 'en' for English) hosting the article.

title.description: The display title of the retrieved Wikipedia page.

pageid.description: The unique numeric identifier of the Wikipedia page.

summary.description: A brief lead paragraph summarizing the article topic.

text.description: The complete article text including all sections.

url.description: The canonical web URL of the Wikipedia page.

sections.description: Hierarchical list of article sections with headings and nested subsections.

level.description: Nesting depth of the section (1 for top-level).

text.description: Full wikitext content of the section.

tools.name: get_summary
tools.description: Retrieve a concise summary extract and basic metadata for a Wikipedia article.

title.description: The exact Wikipedia page title whose summary is requested.

language.description: The Wikipedia language code (e.g., 'en' for English) hosting the article.

title.description: The display title of the Wikipedia page.

extract.description: A concise plain-text summary of the article topic.

description.description: A short subtitle or description of the page topic.

thumbnail.description: Thumbnail image metadata (url, width, height) if available.

lang.description: The language code of the returned summary.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the summary was last updated.

tools.name: get_page_sections
tools.description: Retrieve the hierarchical section headings and corresponding text fragments of a Wikipedia article.

title.description: The exact Wikipedia page title whose section outline is requested.

language.description: The Wikipedia language code (e.g., 'en' for English) hosting the article.

title.description: The display title of the Wikipedia page.

sections.description: Ordered list of article sections with their heading level and text content.

level.description: Nesting depth of the section (1 for top-level).

tools.name: get_related_pages
tools.description: Retrieve a list of Wikipedia pages thematically related to a given article.

title.description: The exact Wikipedia page title whose related pages are requested.

limit.description: Maximum number of related pages to return (default 10).

language.description: The Wikipedia language code (e.g., 'en' for English) hosting the article.

title.description: The display title of the source Wikipedia page.

related.description: List of Wikipedia pages thematically linked to the source page.

title.description: The display title of the related Wikipedia page.

pageid.description: The unique numeric identifier of the related Wikipedia page.

extract.description: A brief summary extract of the related page.

url.description: The canonical web URL of the related Wikipedia page.
